task.prompt: Write a function `compact_data(data)` that removes empty values from nested data.

tests.code:
result = compact_data({'x': [1, None, (2, None, {'y': None, 'z': 3})]})
assert result == {'x': [1, (2, {'z': 3})]}
assert isinstance(result['x'], list)
assert isinstance(result['x'][1], tuple)

assert compact_data(None) is None
assert compact_data('') == ''

gold_spec.goal: Recursively remove values that are exactly None from nested Python data while preserving structure.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: a scalar, dict, list, or tuple containing nested supported values

outputs.name: cleaned
outputs.description: data with None values removed from containers

preconditions: Containers are ordinary dict, list, and tuple instances.
Only container traversal is required; all other values are returned as values.
postconditions: Remove dict entries whose value is exactly None before or after recursion.
Remove list and tuple elements that are exactly None.
Recurse into dict values, list elements, and tuple elements that remain.
Preserve container type: lists return lists and tuples return tuples.
Keep falsey non-None values such as 0, False, empty string, empty dict, empty list, and empty tuple.
Do not mutate the input object or any nested container.
invariants: No returned container should contain an element or dict value that is exactly None.
edge_cases: A top-level None returns None because there is no parent container to remove it from.
acceptance_criteria: The result has the same recursive container shape except for removed None values.
The implementation never converts tuples to lists or drops other falsey values.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt says 'empty values', which commonly causes models to remove all falsey values, mutate in place, or collapse tuples into lists during recursion.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: missing exact removal predicate, recursive container-type preservation, and non-mutation semantics
